title: Pre-market action: Here's the trade setup for today's session
summary: Indian equity markets experienced a significant sell-off on Monday, driven by escalating geopolitical tensions and a surge in crude oil prices. Investors are now closely watching developments in West Asia, as uncertainty surrounding the conflict is expected to maintain cautious sentiment in the near term.

title: Stocks in news: IndiGo, RIL, Torrent Power, ideaForge, NTPC Green
summary: Indian markets experienced a sharp sell-off, extending the downtrend amid global concerns and geopolitical tensions. Nifty faces immediate support at 23,500, with resistance expected around 24,000-24,300. IndiGo flights faced airspace issues, while Reliance Retail acquired 'Pahadi Local'. Torrent Power raised Rs 2,000 crore via NCDs, and ideaForge began drone training for NATO personnel.

title: Dalal Street sinks to 10-month low amid panic selling as crude crosses $100
summary: Indian stock markets experienced a significant downturn on Monday. Key indices reached a 10-month low as oil prices surged past $100 a barrel. This rise, linked to the West Asia conflict, fueled inflation worries. Foreign investors sold shares heavily. Domestic investors bought shares. The market saw widespread selling pressure across various sectors.

title: Edelweiss raises Rs 375 crore in alternatives arm
summary: Edelweiss Financial Services has secured fresh capital for its alternatives arm, EAAA India Alternatives, selling a 4.4% stake for ₹375 crore. This move precedes the platform's planned listing in India's private credit and real assets sector, with EAAA currently managing approximately ₹68,175 crore in assets.

title: Sebi settles cases with 29 venture capital funds
summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India has resolved cases with 29 venture capital funds. These funds faced allegations of violating securities laws. They have agreed to pay a total of approximately '2 crore. The settlements address non-compliance with older rules regarding venture capital funds. This involved holding investments beyond the permitted liquidation period.

title: Sundaram Alternates floats hybrid fund, to raise Rs 1,500 crore
summary: Sundaram Alternates is launching a hybrid private credit and equity fund targeting ₹1,500 crore, aiming for 18-20% returns. The fund will allocate 51-60% to listed equities and 40-49% to high-yield private credit, focusing on mid-market businesses and real estate opportunities.

title: Bank stocks slip as Maha farm loan waiver adds to FPI worry
summary: Banking stocks plunged on Monday, with the Bank Nifty seeing its steepest fall since April 2025. Maharashtra's farm loan waiver announcement triggered widespread selling, particularly impacting state-owned banks. This, coupled with foreign investors' cautious stance on Indian equities, led to significant losses across the banking sector, with Union Bank of India and IDFC First Bank among the hardest hit.

title: Sensex & Nifty’s resilience masks steeper selloff in broader market
summary: Indian stock markets saw a significant downturn since September 2024. Many top companies experienced sharp declines. Over 700 NSE-listed stocks fell, with a large portion dropping over 20%. Smallcap and Microcap indices also registered substantial losses. This indicates a growing risk aversion in the market's lower segments.

title: Higher input costs likely to erode chemical companies' profits
summary: Rising US-Iran tensions are impacting Indian chemical makers. Higher crude oil prices are increasing input costs for essential materials like methanol. Shipping delays and rerouting are adding to expenses. This situation affects companies reliant on imports from the Gulf. The fertilizer industry also faces supply shortages. These factors are squeezing profit margins for many Indian chemical and fertilizer producers.

title: Sebi chief urges investors to stay calm amid global jitters
summary: Sebi Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey advised investors to stay calm amid global market volatility, emphasizing India's strong domestic fundamentals. He highlighted the increasing participation of domestic investors, including individuals and mutual funds, in equities, now holding 36% of Nifty 50 free-float market capitalization. Meanwhile, NSE plans to appoint investment bankers for its IPO this month.

title: Crude surge triggers 9% fall in Nifty 50 in 2026; past trends suggest relief once oil cools
summary: Despite a nearly 9% fall in Nifty 50 due to surging crude oil prices in 2026, historical data suggests equities typically recover within two months as oil prices cool. Past instances show Nifty rebounding after sharp oil spikes, indicating a potential for similar recovery as crude prices stabilize.

title: Stock Radar: Solar Industries stock breaks above falling trendline resistance; time to buy?
summary: Solar Industries Ltd, a defense stock, has broken out from a falling trendline resistance on daily charts, signaling potential upside. Experts suggest buying for a target of Rs 15,600 in 3-4 weeks if momentum sustains. The stock has shown strong recent performance, rallying over 22% in three months.

title: Like banks, leadership rotation rule may apply to large NBFCs
summary: The matter was discussed at a meeting involving the RBI, government officials and representatives from the NBFC sector at the department of financial services on February 26. While Section 10 of the Banking Regulation Act mandates banks to follow the rotation policy at the top management and staff levels, there is no such guideline for NBFCs.

title: Impact on credit culture: One good deed could sow seeds for many bad loans
summary: The Maharashtra government fulfilled its poll promise waiving loans up to Rs 2 lakh and also offering a Rs 50,000 incentive for farmers regularly paying their loans. The government has earmarked Rs 20,000 crore for the waiver and Rs 15,000 crore for the incentive for non-defaulters. The decision is estimated to benefit 5 million farmers that have outstanding crop loan dues up to September 30, 2025.

title: IRDAI nod for Allianz Jio Reinsurance and Kiwi General
summary: IRDAI has issued Certificates of Registration to Allianz Jio Reinsurance and Kiwi General Insurance, granting them final approval to begin operations. Allianz Jio Reinsurance is a joint venture between Allianz Group and Jio Financial Services, while Kiwi General Insurance is supported by WestBridge Capital and former Tata AIG chief Neelesh Garg.

title: Life insurers' new biz rose 18% in Feb
summary: Life insurance new business premium surged 18% in February, propelled by a 24% jump at LIC. This growth is partly attributed to GST tailwinds, making individual life insurance policies cheaper and boosting demand for protection products. The industry saw a significant rise in policies sold and overall premium collection.

title: Indian stock market: 10 things that changed for market overnight- Gift Nifty, US-Iran war, oil prices, Wall Street rally
summary: Gift Nifty was trading around 24,390 level, a premium of nearly 267 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close, indicating a positive start for the Indian stock market indices.

title: GCCs managed to ward off AI impact on hiring—not anymore
summary: Novartis, Celanese, Eli Lilly and Hyundai have opened AI-dedicated centres to explore automation of internal processes and segments. New AI tools that can automate finance, legal, HR, and software development-related tasks threaten the workforce at GCCs.

title: While rivals retreat from default guarantees, Moneyview is doubling down
summary: While peers pivot to co-lending to avoid high costs and regulatory friction, Moneyview’s rising expenses and growing partner list suggest a high-stakes bet on partner-led growth.

title: Ather eyes big distribution push in FY27 adding 400 stores—in stark contrast with Ola Electric's shrunk footprint
summary: Ather Energy plans a big distribution push expanding its store network to over 1,100 by March 2027—opening more than a store every day on an average. Read about the newest warfront in Indian electric scooters market blended with insights from an earlier Ather CEO Tarun Mehta interview.

title: White House prepares executive order to remove Anthropic's Claude AI from federal systems: Axios
summary: The White House is preparing an executive order directing federal agencies to remove Anthropic’s Claude AI from government systems, escalating a legal battle with the AI firm, according to Axios.

title: Law firms field force majeure queries as war sets off panic
summary: The queries to invoke force majeure are rising after the US and Israel’s joint strikes on Iran evoked retaliatory missile and drone attacks across the Persian Gulf region. That has frozen movement through the Strait of Hormuz, sending Brent above $100 a barrel.

title: Can NRI equity investors avail section 115F tax relief even if not claimed earlier?
summary: Two common NRI tax questions answered: how to re-request a failed income-tax refund credited to an NRE account, and whether NRIs can claim capital gains exemption under section 115F if they haven’t used it before.
